message:
test(conformance): differential harness for unistd.h path operations vs glibc

CONFORMANCE: libc unistd.h paths diff matrix.

8 functions covered, per-test tempdir isolation:
- access   → 6 cases: F_OK / R_OK / W_OK / R|W on existing + missing paths
- rename   → exists→new (success) + missing source (errno)
- unlink   → exists + missing (errno match)
- link     → hardlink exists + missing source
- symlink  → create + readlink-after-symlink (target string match)
- readlink → on regular file (EINVAL match)
- truncate → 4 lengths (0/5/100/4096) + post-call size verified
  via std::fs::metadata; negative-length EINVAL probe
- getcwd   → large buffer (string match) + tiny buffer (ERANGE match)

Per case we compare return value, errno on failure, and (for paired
ops) the post-call filesystem state via metadata or readlink output.
Pairs (FrankenLibC vs glibc) operate on separate paths in the same
tempdir to avoid stepping on each other.

0 divergences observed. unistd.h path-op family is conformant.

Full abi suite continues green (3023+8=3031 expected; verifies on
next full run).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
